sourceMovie.title: Companion
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Companion (2025) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Drew Hancock's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Subservience
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Companion excels in horror, Subservience (2024) doubles down on science-fiction to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Upgrade
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Upgrade (2018) is the natural next step after Companion: same science-fiction DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: M3GAN
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Companion who respond to its horror will find M3GAN (2022) immediately familiar. Both films share a science-fiction sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Countdown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Companion fans keep landing on Countdown (2019) — the horror is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Outside the Wire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Outside the Wire draws from the same science-fiction well as Companion, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2021 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Archive
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Archive (2020) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that Companion later expanded. If Companion resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: Wifelike
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The science-fiction appeal of Companion — that tense edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into Wifelike (2022). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Life Like
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Life Like (2019) clearly understood what makes science-fiction work — the same quality that keeps Companion rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Artifice Girl
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Artifice Girl (2023) is the kind of film Companion fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The science-fiction overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Her
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Her shares Companion's gift for science-fiction — the 2013 film delivers romance without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after Companion?
faq.answer: Try Subservience, Upgrade, M3GAN—each shares DNA with Companion while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Companion?
faq.answer: Companion (2025) is best described as Horror, Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Companion worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
